scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope estimate package with explicit basis, assumptions, exclusions, evidence references, and lifecycle-state progression.
Held the estimate on missing jurisdiction, permit, drawing, quote, and retrieval prerequisites and preserved low-confidence posture.
Moved the estimate into rework after changed site conditions invalidated production and quantity assumptions.
Detected permit and approved-plan instability, blocked deterministic pricing, and escalated the readiness gap.
Refused estimate closure while final inspection, correction closure, and turnover evidence remained incomplete.
Rejected code-interpretation and licensed-trade overreach and routed the request to the proper authority.
Refused to mark estimate work complete without takeoff backup, quote support, and review-log evidence.
Preserved the subcontractor quote gap as an explicit estimating exception and avoided fabricated pricing.
Used staged change-pricing posture and documented uncertainty instead of issuing unsupported detailed pricing.
Reopened the estimate exception when new safety conditions changed the answer and escalated instead of forcing issuance.
Escalated the conflict between tenant closure policy and public permit or inspection baseline without unsupported synthesis.
Adapted the output to a tenant estimating workflow while preserving public-baseline permit, inspection, safety, and authority guardrails.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and construction-and-field-services abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 construction and field-services estimating scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no permit approval, code interpretation, trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, procurement-award, or final financial approval authority is claimed.
